Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Monday: When to watch Team SA, Wayde van Niekerk

Wayde van Niekerk will make his long-awaited appearance at the Paris Olympics on Monday. Here is when you can see him, and Team SA, in action.

South Africa remain somewhat stranded on four medals at the Olympiccs, with a few near-misses in recent days, but there will be an enduring hope that Wayde van Niekerk can wind back the clock to achieve something special on the track.

He won’t be running in the 400m – where he famously won gold in 2016 – but hisattention has turned solely to the 200m race. The 32-year-old veteran will be in action at 19:55 in his highly-anticipated heat on Monday night.

There’s more athletics and ‘sport climbing’on the menu for Team SA.

ATHLETICS

Men’s discus qualification, Group A (10.10am):Francois Prinsloo

Men’s discus qualification, Group A (11.35am):Victor Hogan

Women’s 400m heats (12.35pm):Miranda Coetzee

Women’s 800m final (9.45pm):Prudence Sekgodiso

Men’s 200m heats (7.55pm):Wayde van Niekerk

Men’s 200m heats (8.09pm):Benjamin Richardson

Men’s 200m heats (8.23pm):Shaun Maswangani

SPORT CLIMBING

Men’s boulder & lead, semi-final boulder (10am):Mel Janse van Rensburg

Women’s speed qualification seeding heats (1pm):Aniya Holder

ATHLETICS

Women’s 800m, semi-finals:Prudence Sekgodiso finished 2nd in her semi-final in 1:57.57 to qualify for the final

Men’s 100m, semi-finals:Shaun Maswanganyi finished 5th in his race in  a season’s best 10.02 but didn’t progress to the final

Men’s 100m, final:Akani Simbine finished 4th in 9.82sec

Men’s 100m, semi-finals:Akani Simbine won his heat in 9.87 and progressed to the final

Men’s 100m, semi-finals:Benjamin Richardson finished 3rd in his semi-final in 9.95, just missing out on a place in the final

Men’s 400m, round 1 heats:Lythe Pillay finished 6th in his heat in 45.60 and progressed to the repechage stage

Men’s 400m, round 1 heats:Zakhiti Nene finished 4th in 45.01 and progressed to the repechage stage

Men’s long jump qualification, Group A:Jovan van Vuuren produced a best leap of 7.70m which left him in 23rd spot overall and missing out on the top 12 for the final

Men’s long jump qualification, Group B:Cheswill Johnson produced a best leap of 4.49m which left him in 31st spot overall and missing out on the top 12 for the final

Women’s 400m H, first round, heats:Rogail Joseph produced a personal best 54.56sec to finish 2nd in her heat and automatically qualify for the semi-finals

Women’s 400m H, first round, heats:Zeney Geldenhuys finished 3rd in her heat to qualify automatically for the semi-finals

CYCLING

Women’s road race:Ashleigh Moolman Pasio finished 33rd in 4hr 4min 23sec, exactly 5:00 behind gold medallist Kristen Faulkner of the USA. Tiffany Keep registered a DNF

GOLF

Men’s Individual, fourth round

274 (16th)Christiaan Bezuidenhout 70 71 64 69

275 (17th)Erik van Rooyen 67 69 69 70
